District of ColumbiaThe Longshoremen’s and Harbor Workers’ Compensation Act [this chapter] was made applicable in respect to the injury or death of an employee of an employer carrying on any employment in the District of Columbia, by , , as amended.act May 17, 1928, ch. 61245 Stat. 600

children.heading: Disability or death; injuries occurring upon navigable waters of United States
children.label: a Disability or death; injuries occurring upon navigable waters of United States
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, compensation shall be payable under this chapter in respect of disability or death of an employee, but only if the disability or death results from an injury occurring upon the navigable waters of the United States (including any adjoining pier, wharf, dry dock, terminal, building way, marine railway, or other adjoining area customarily used by an employer in loading, unloading, repairing, dismantling, or building a vessel).

children.heading: Governmental officers and employees
children.label: b Governmental officers and employees
children.text: No compensation shall be payable in respect of the disability or death of an officer or employee of the United States, or any agency thereof, or of any State or foreign government, or any subdivision thereof.

children.heading: Intoxication; willful intention to kill
children.label: c Intoxication; willful intention to kill
children.text: No compensation shall be payable if the injury was occasioned solely by the intoxication of the employee or by the willful intention of the employee to injure or kill himself or another.

No compensation shall be payable to an employee employed at a facility of an employer if, as certified by the Secretary, the facility is engaged in the business of building, repairing, or dismantling exclusively small vessels (as defined in paragraph (3) of this subsection), unless the injury occurs while upon the navigable waters of the United States or while upon any adjoining pier, wharf, dock, facility over land for launching vessels, or facility over land for hauling, lifting, or drydocking vessels.

children.label: A
children.text: who is employed at a facility which is used in the business of building, repairing, or dismantling small vessels if such facility receives Federal maritime subsidies; or

children.label: B
children.text: if the employee is not subject to coverage under a State workers’ compensation law.

children.label: A
children.text: a commercial barge which is under 900 lightship displacement tons; or

children.label: B
children.text: a commercial tugboat, towboat, crew boat, supply boat, fishing vessel, or other work vessel which is under 1,600 tons gross as measured under , or an alternate tonnage measured under section 14302 of that title as prescribed by the Secretary under section 14104 of that title.section 14502 of title 46

children.heading: Credit for benefits paid under other laws
children.label: e Credit for benefits paid under other laws
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any amounts paid to an employee for the same injury, disability, or death for which benefits are claimed under this chapter pursuant to any other workers’ compensation law or shall be credited against any liability imposed by this chapter.section 30104 of title 46
